stories: Echo Global Logistics launched EchoParcel on May 14 2026 via FedEx partnership within EchoShip platform, covering parcel+LTL+TL after ITS Logistics acquisition close
NRF forecasts May 2026 US container imports at 2.13M TEU +9.3% YoY but H1 2026 total down 2% YoY due to tariff base effect from April 2025
Hertz formed Oro Mobility as AV asset management platform supporting Uber robotaxi program with Nuro/Lucid vehicles, SF Bay Area launch late 2026
Torc Robotics VP detailed path to driver-out highway deployments late 2026, commercial scale 2027, on Freightliner Cascadia with Daimler Truck backing
FMCSA published Dalilah's Law final rule tightening CDL eligibility standards and planned ANPRM on female driver anti-harassment protections May 2026
Bound Logistics LLC Florida carrier with 57 drivers filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in District of New Jersey May 2026
Flexport targeting 30% revenue growth and IPO readiness in 2026; BlackRock-managed funds committing $250M to nearly double supply chain lending capacity
FedEx board approved spinoff of FedEx Freight as standalone NYSE-listed FDXF company with ~$9B revenue and 30K trucks, separation set for June 1 2026
Volvo Autonomous Solutions and DSV launched commercial AV freight on Dallas-Houston corridor using Aurora's platform on May 13 2026 with safety driver, targeting driverless H2 2026
Uber Freight confirmed job cuts in commercial org following D'Andrae Larry appointment as CCO; first-tender acceptance dropped from 92% to 85%
C.H. Robinson reported using generative AI agents to complete over 3 million shipping-related tasks in brokerage operations
California DMV adopted regulations lifting ban on heavy-duty autonomous vehicles requiring 500K miles supervised testing before driverless operation
FTR Trucking Conditions Index hit 4-year high in February 2026; diesel at $5.64/gal +54% YoY creating carrier cost headwind in May 2026
FreightWaves analysis confirmed May 2026 load board surge is tariff front-loading by shippers pulling inventory forward, not structural demand recovery
FMCSA Motus carrier registration portal launched May 14 2026 replacing legacy system with improved fraud prevention and streamlined onboarding

Supreme Court ruled 9-0 in Montgomery v. Caribe Transport II on May 14 2026 that freight brokers can be sued under state law for negligent carrier hiring, stripping federal preemption shield brokers relied on for decades

Amazon launched Supply Chain Services (ASCS) on May 4 2026, opening its multimodal freight, warehousing, and last-mile network to all businesses including P&G, 3M, Lands' End, and American Eagle Outfitters
Kodiak AI began autonomous freight hauling with Roehl Transport on Dallas-Houston corridor (4x roundtrip/week) in May 2026, targeting driver-out by end of 2026 and commercial scale 2027
GXO Logistics raised its 2026 outlook after strong Q1, citing 35% sequential North America pipeline growth, 20,000+ robots deployed globally, and humanoid robot pilots underway in California
FedEx Freight board approved spinoff on May 12 2026; $9B LTL carrier becomes independent FDXF on NYSE June 1 with 4-6% revenue growth target and 12% operating margin
DAT spot rates: dry van $2.37/mile up ~26% YoY, flatbed $3.05/mile near multi-year high; reefer load-to-truck 16.4 (up from 14.5); CH Robinson raised dry van cost/mile forecast to +23% YoY
Industry study found 83% of freight and logistics leaders operate in reactive mode due to system fragmentation despite widespread AI tool adoption

Post-Roadcheck week ending May 15 saw reefer spot rates post their largest week-over-week spike ever recorded and dry van near-record, with total load activity +21% WoW and Market Demand Index at 5-year high
5,100+ freight-related layoffs hit US supply chain on May 18 as FreshRealm filed Chapter 11, GEODIS, Ryder, and DSV each shed workers in multiple states after losing customer contracts
FreightWaves found mid-market 3PL brokers under working capital strain as carrier rates firm faster than shipper contracts, creating shakeout of static-pricing brokers amid peak market tightness

Waabi differentiating from Aurora with door-to-door facility-to-facility AV capability via surface street navigation, targeting driver-out 2026 with $1B raised January 2026 and Volvo manufacturing partner

Fr8Technologies formalized agentic AI transformation framework reporting 15x domestic and 5x cross-border productivity gains, signaling AI-native platforms eliminating manual freight broker work at scale

FMCSA published second broker transparency NPRM with 48-hour electronic records delivery mandate covering rate confirmations and shipper invoices, invalidating pre-signed waiver clauses in broker-carrier agreements

Project44 launched Autopilot on May 11 2026, a no-code AI agent platform for supply chain procurement and exception management backed by 259,000-carrier data graph with early adopters reporting 4% freight spend reduction

Spot-contract rate spread compressed to approximately 11 cents/mile from 39 cents a year ago, with J.B. Hunt projecting high-single to low-double-digit intermodal contract rate increases, signaling imminent multi-mode repricing
XPO reported record Q1 2026 results with $2.10B revenue (+7.7% YoY), $101M net income, and LTL adjusted OR improved 200bps to 83.9 on April 30 2026
STG PE firm acquired Carrier Logistics Inc. in April 2026 to build AI-native LTL platform with agentic dispatch, real-time routing, and terminal optimization
Domestic intermodal spot rates at $1.39/mile vs trucking $2.80/mile represent a closing 50% arbitrage window as J.B. Hunt projects imminent intermodal contract rate increases and volumes grow 12% YoY
Aurora completed 1,000-mile Fort Worth-Phoenix driverless freight run in approximately 15 hours, half the time legally allowed under HOS rules, with fleet targeting 200+ trucks by year-end 2026

Section 301 tariff cliff on July 24 2026 replacing Section 122 10% Chinese goods tariffs creates 5-week import front-loading surge window driving elevated drayage and truckload demand into an already-tight market
